headline: SILVER BELOW $77, LOWEST IN A WEEK AS DOLLAR RALLIES ON WARSH FED HEARING
snippet: Silver extended losses to below $77/oz on April 21 — its lowest level in a week — pressured by a strengthening dollar as investors weighed U.S.–Iran ceasefire uncertainty and the Senate confirmation hearing for Fed Chair nominee Kevin Warsh. Despite the dip, silver has surged more than 150% over the past year as industrial and investment demand combine.

headline: $2.2B ALL-SHARE DEAL CREATES GUYANA GOLD GIANT — 500,000 OZ/YR TARGET IN PLAY
snippet: G Mining Ventures Corp. agreed to acquire G2 Goldfields Inc. in an all-share transaction valued at approximately $2.2 billion, combining two adjacent assets in Guyana into a single district-scale complex targeting more than 500,000 ounces of gold annually. The deal highlights the accelerating consolidation of high-grade junior gold assets as elevated prices make M&A math compelling.

headline: 4,896 g/t SILVER AT GALENA: AMERICAS GOLD LAUNCHES RECORD 64,000m DRILL PROGRAM
snippet: Americas Gold and Silver Corporation discovered ten new high-grade silver-copper-antimony veins at its Galena Complex in Idaho, including an intercept of 4,896 g/t silver and 3.95% copper over 1.3 metres. The company is now launching its largest-ever exploration program — 64,000 metres across both the Galena Complex and its Cosalá operations.

headline: CORE LITHIUM RESTARTS FINNISS AS ENERGY STORAGE BOOM RESURRECTS BEATEN-DOWN LITHIUM
snippet: Australia's Core Lithium will restart its Finniss mine from May, with first concentrate delivery targeted for late 2026, as an energy-storage demand surge reverses years of producer shutdowns. Australia's total lithium export loadings hit a record 488,200 tonnes in March 2026 — a signal the sector's recovery is broadening beyond individual company restarts.

headline: NEXGEN TO BREAK GROUND ON CANADA'S BIGGEST URANIUM DEPOSIT THIS SUMMER
snippet: NexGen Energy is set to begin construction this summer on the Rook uranium mine in northern Saskatchewan — Canada's largest development-stage uranium deposit — as spot prices resurge above $100/lb and utilities scramble to contract supply. Denison Mines' nearby Phoenix project also entered construction, the first fully permitted uranium mine to break ground in Canada in over a decade.

headline: GOLDMINING DRILLS FIRST HOLES AT SÃO JORGE IN BRAZIL'S TAPAJÓS GOLD BELT
snippet: GoldMining Inc. has commenced its 2026 exploration drill program at the São Jorge Project in Brazil's Tapajós gold district, targeting resource expansion at one of the region's most prospective undeveloped gold assets. The Tapajós has drawn renewed institutional interest as Brazilian gold exploration activity accelerates alongside record gold prices.

headline: SILVER HEADS FOR 6TH STRAIGHT ANNUAL DEFICIT IN 2026, PHYSICAL INVESTMENT DEMAND SURGES 20%
snippet: The Silver Institute's 2026 World Silver Survey projects global physical silver investment will jump 20% to a three-year high of 227 million ounces as Western retail demand recovers alongside surging industrial usage. Despite total supply reaching a decade high of 1.05 billion ounces, the market will record its sixth consecutive annual structural deficit.

headline: AI BUILDING BOOM MEANS DATA CENTRES ALONE WILL CONSUME 475,000 TONNES OF COPPER IN 2026
snippet: JPMorgan estimates that data-centre buildout driven by AI will consume approximately 475,000 metric tonnes of copper in 2026 alone — a demand source that barely existed five years ago — while the bank also projects a total refined copper market shortfall of roughly 330,000 metric tonnes for the year. The structural deficit underpins the case for copper explorers and developers even as near-term prices gyrate on geopolitical noise.
